title: 关灯后的房间
coverQuote: After I turn off the lights, the room doesn't go completely dark. The streetlight outside paints a thin orange stripe on the wall.

sentences.en: I turn off the desk lamp. The room does not go completely dark.
sentences.zh: 我关了台灯。房间没有完全变黑。

sentences.en: The streetlight outside my window paints a thin orange stripe on the wall above my bed.
sentences.zh: 窗外的路灯在床头上方的墙上画了一道细细的橙色条纹。

sentences.en: I lie on my back and watch the stripe. It does not move. It is just there, keeping me company.
sentences.zh: 我仰面躺着，看着那道条纹。它不动。它就在那儿，陪着我。

sentences.en: I can hear the neighbor's TV through the wall. A laugh track. Then another.
sentences.zh: 能隔着墙听到邻居的电视声。一阵笑声罐头。又一阵。

sentences.en: The orange stripe will be there every night. I have never thanked it.
sentences.zh: 那道橙色条纹每晚都在。我从来没谢过它。

sentences.en: I close my eyes. The stripe is still there behind my eyelids, a warm orange glow.
sentences.zh: 我闭上眼睛。那道条纹还在眼皮后面，一片暖橙色的光。

title: 深夜洗衣房
coverQuote: The laundromat at midnight. Just me and the rhythmic thump of someone else's clothes in the dryer.

sentences.en: I forgot to do laundry during the day so here I am at midnight, pouring detergent into the cap.
sentences.zh: 我白天忘了洗衣服，所以半夜在这里，把洗衣液倒进瓶盖里。

sentences.en: The washing machine starts its cycle with a low hum. Water rushing in. Then the drum begins to turn.
sentences.zh: 洗衣机开始运转，发出低沉的嗡鸣。水灌进去。然后滚筒开始转。

sentences.en: The laundromat at midnight. Just me and the rhythmic thump of someone else's clothes in the dryer.
sentences.zh: 深夜洗衣房。只有我和烘干机里别人衣服有节奏的拍打声。

sentences.en: I sit on the plastic chair and open my phone. No signal in the basement. So I just watch the machines.
sentences.zh: 我坐在塑料椅子上打开手机。地下室里没信号。所以我只是看着机器。

sentences.en: The dryer next to mine is empty. Its door is slightly open. A single forgotten sock is stuck to the drum.
sentences.zh: 我旁边的烘干机是空的。门半开着。一只被遗忘的袜子粘在滚筒上。

sentences.en: The spin cycle ends. Silence for three seconds. Then the rinse water drains out with a gurgle.
sentences.zh: 甩干结束了。安静了三秒。然后漂洗水咕噜咕噜地排出去。

title: 雨打在咖啡馆天窗上
coverQuote: Rain on the skylight sounds different than rain on a window. Softer. Higher up. Like the sky is tapping to come in.

sentences.en: The coffee shop I go to has a small skylight above the back corner. Most people do not notice it.
sentences.zh: 我去的那家咖啡店后角上方有一个小天窗。大多数人注意不到。

sentences.en: Today it is raining. The drops hit the glass in an irregular pattern, like someone who does not know how to type.
sentences.zh: 今天在下雨。雨滴以不规则的节奏打在玻璃上，像一个不会打字的人在敲键盘。

sentences.en: Rain on the skylight sounds different than rain on a window. Softer. Higher up. Like the sky is tapping to come in.
sentences.zh: 雨打在天窗上的声音和打在窗户上不一样。更轻。更高。像天空在轻轻敲门想进来。

sentences.en: I order a flat white and take the corner seat. The barista knows my order but still asks every time.
sentences.zh: 我点了一杯澳白，坐了角落位置。咖啡师知道我要什么，但每次都还是问一遍。

sentences.en: A woman at the next table is sketching in a small notebook. Her pencil moves in quick short strokes, like the rain above.
sentences.zh: 隔壁桌的女人在一个小本子上画画。她的铅笔快速短促地动着，像头顶的雨。

sentences.en: I stay until the rain stops. The sudden silence after rain is its own kind of sound.
sentences.zh: 我一直待到雨停。雨后的突然安静，本身也是一种声音。

title: 公交车站等雨停
coverQuote: The bus shelter roof leaks in one spot. A single drip lands on the same cracked tile every three seconds.

sentences.en: The rain started while I was halfway to the bus stop. Not a drizzle. The serious kind.
sentences.zh: 我走到半路雨就下起来了。不是毛毛雨。是认真的那种。

sentences.en: I duck under the bus shelter. Three other people are already there. We arrange ourselves at equal distances.
sentences.zh: 我钻进公交站的遮雨棚。已经有三个人在里面了。我们以等距排列好自己。

sentences.en: The bus shelter roof leaks in one spot. A single drip lands on the same cracked tile every three seconds.
sentences.zh: 公交站遮雨棚有一个地方漏水。一滴水每三秒落在同一块裂开的瓷砖上。

sentences.en: A kid in a yellow raincoat jumps in a puddle nearby. His mother pulls him back by the hood.
sentences.zh: 一个穿黄色雨衣的小孩在旁边跳进水坑里。他妈妈揪着帽子把他拉回来。

sentences.en: The bus arrives seven minutes late. We board in single file, shaking off umbrellas and jacket sleeves.
sentences.zh: 公交车晚了七分钟到。我们排成一列上车，抖着雨伞和袖子。

sentences.en: I find a window seat. The glass is fogged. I wipe a circle with my palm and watch the wet city slide past.
sentences.zh: 我找到一个窗边的位置。玻璃上都是雾。我用手掌擦出一个圆圈，看着湿漉漉的城市滑过去。

title: 下雨的星期一下午
coverQuote: A rainy Monday afternoon is the world's way of telling you to slow down. The traffic outside agrees.

sentences.en: It started raining at 2pm and has not stopped. The sky is a solid sheet of gray.
sentences.zh: 下午两点开始下雨，一直没停。天空是一整块灰色。

sentences.en: A rainy Monday afternoon is the world's way of telling you to slow down. The traffic outside agrees. It is crawling.
sentences.zh: 下雨的星期一下午，是世界在告诉你慢下来。外面的车流表示同意。它正在爬行。

sentences.en: I make a second cup of tea. Chamomile this time. The first was green tea, but that was two hours ago.
sentences.zh: 我泡了第二杯茶。这次是洋甘菊。第一杯是绿茶，但那是两个小时前的事了。

sentences.en: My laptop screen dims to save power. I touch the trackpad to wake it up. The cursor blinks on an empty document.
sentences.zh: 笔记本电脑屏幕暗了省电。我碰了一下触控板唤醒它。光标在一个空白文档里闪烁。

sentences.en: Outside the window, a pigeon lands on the railing. It shakes water off its wings and stares at me through the glass.
sentences.zh: 窗外，一只鸽子落在栏杆上。它抖掉翅膀上的水，透过玻璃盯着我。

sentences.en: I should be working. But the rain and the pigeon and the second cup of tea have formed a coalition against productivity.
sentences.zh: 我应该在工作。但雨、鸽子和第二杯茶已经组成了一个反生产力联盟。

title: 雨中等出租车
coverQuote: Waiting for a taxi in the rain is a small exercise in hope. Every pair of headlights could be yours.

sentences.en: It is raining and I forgot my umbrella. I am standing under the narrow awning of a closed bakery.
sentences.zh: 下雨了，我忘了带伞。我站在一家关了门的面包店窄窄的雨棚下。

sentences.en: Waiting for a taxi in the rain is a small exercise in hope. Every pair of headlights could be yours. Most are not.
sentences.zh: 在雨中等出租车是一种小小的希望练习。每一对车灯都可能是你的。大多数不是。

sentences.en: I hold my phone out and try to book a ride. The app says: high demand. Estimated wait: 18 minutes.
sentences.zh: 我拿出手机试着叫车。软件显示：需求高峰。预计等待：18分钟。

sentences.en: A taxi splashes through a puddle three meters away. I step back too late. My shoes are wet now.
sentences.zh: 一辆出租车从三米外的水坑开过去溅起水花。我往后躲，已经晚了。鞋子湿了。

sentences.en: The bakery awning drips steadily onto my shoulder. I shift half a step to the left.
sentences.zh: 面包店雨棚上的水滴滴到我的肩膀上。我往左挪了半步。

sentences.en: Finally a green light on my phone. A car is coming. I step out from under the awning and wave at a pair of headlights.
sentences.zh: 终于手机上亮了一个绿灯。有车来了。我从雨棚下走出来，朝一对车灯挥手。

title: 便利店门口的雨天
coverQuote: The convenience store doorway is the unofficial shelter for people who forgot their umbrellas.

sentences.en: I step into the convenience store to buy an umbrella. They are sold out.
sentences.zh: 我走进便利店想买一把伞。卖完了。

sentences.en: So I buy a can of coffee and stand in the doorway, watching the rain. I am not the only one.
sentences.zh: 所以我买了一罐咖啡，站在门口看雨。我不是唯一一个这么做的。

sentences.en: The convenience store doorway is the unofficial shelter for people who forgot their umbrellas. We are a small, quiet club.
sentences.zh: 便利店门口是忘带伞的人的非官方避雨所。我们是一个小小的安静俱乐部。

sentences.en: A man in a suit runs past, holding a newspaper over his head. The newspaper is dissolving.
sentences.zh: 一个穿西装的男人跑过去，把报纸举在头上。报纸正在融化。

sentences.en: The rain slows down after ten minutes. One by one, the doorway club members leave. I stay until my coffee is finished.
sentences.zh: 十分钟后雨小了。门口俱乐部的人一个接一个走了。我喝完咖啡才走。

title: 雨天晾衣阳台
coverQuote: I forgot to bring the laundry in before the rain. Now my shirt is wetter than when I put it in the washer.

sentences.en: I hear the rain start and remember my laundry.
sentences.zh: 我听到雨声，想起了晾的衣服。

sentences.en: I run to the balcony. Too late.
sentences.zh: 我跑到阳台上。太晚了。

sentences.en: My shirt is wetter than when I put it in the washer.
sentences.zh: 我的衬衫比放进洗衣机的时候还湿。

sentences.en: I leave it there. It will dry again tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 我就让它挂在那。明天还会干的。

sentences.en: Rain on clean clothes smells different from rain on dirty ones. Cleaner.
sentences.zh: 干净衣服上的雨水和脏衣服上的味道不一样。更干净。

title: 地铁口的雨伞摊
coverQuote: As soon as the rain starts, the umbrella sellers appear at the subway exit like mushrooms after a storm.

sentences.en: The rain starts the moment I reach the top of the subway stairs. A perfect, inconvenient timing.
sentences.zh: 我刚走到地铁楼梯顶上，雨就下起来了。一个完美的、不合时宜的时间点。

sentences.en: As soon as the rain starts, the umbrella sellers appear at the subway exit like mushrooms after a storm. They unfold their wares on the wet ground.
sentences.zh: 雨一开始下，卖伞的就出现在地铁口，像暴风雨后的蘑菇。他们在湿地上摆开货品。

sentences.en: Ten yuan for a flimsy black umbrella. The kind that will break in the first strong wind. I buy one anyway.
sentences.zh: 十块钱一把薄薄的黑伞。那种第一次大风就会坏掉的。我还是买了一把。

sentences.en: The seller hands it to me without a word. No eye contact. He is already looking for the next customer.
sentences.zh: 卖伞的把伞递给我，没说话。没眼神接触。他已经在找下一个顾客了。

sentences.en: I open the umbrella and step into the rain. It works. For now, that is enough.
sentences.zh: 我撑开伞走进雨里。它撑住了。暂时来说，这就够了。

title: 秋雨夜的房间
coverQuote: The first autumn rain sounds different. It has more weight. Each drop lands like it means it.

sentences.en: The first autumn rain of the year arrives at 10pm. I know it is autumn rain because of the sound.
sentences.zh: 今年第一场秋雨在晚上十点来了。我知道是秋雨，因为声音不一样。

sentences.en: Autumn rain sounds heavier than summer rain. Each drop lands like it means it. The window frame vibrates slightly.
sentences.zh: 秋雨的声音比夏雨更沉。每一滴落下来都像是认真的。窗框在微微震动。

sentences.en: I pull my blanket up to my chin and leave the window open a crack. The cold air mixes with the warm room air.
sentences.zh: 我把毯子拉到下巴，窗户留了一条缝。冷空气和房间里的暖空气混在一起。

sentences.en: My cat jumps onto the bed and curls into a circle near my feet. She also knows it is autumn rain.
sentences.zh: 我的猫跳上床，在我脚边蜷成一团。她也知道这是秋雨。

sentences.en: I do not look at my phone. I just listen. The rain has a rhythm if you pay attention long enough.
sentences.zh: 我不看手机。我只是听着。如果你听的时间够长，雨是有节奏的。
